ai.runtime.riskLevelDenied: Error: Current risk level "{riskLevel}" does not allow {opType} operation. Please increase ai.risk_level in settings and try again.
ai.runtime.sensitiveRejected: Error: Path "{filePath}" points to a sensitive file, rejected.
ai.runtime.queuedForApproval: [Pending approval] Queued for approval, task ID: {taskId} ({desc}). Go to the Approvals tab to approve or reject; will not execute until approved.
ai.tool.listFiles.desc: List all files and subdirectories under a specified project path. Returns each entry's name, type (file/directory), size in bytes, and modification time. Use to understand project structure and file organization.
ai.tool.listFiles.dirPath: Path relative to the project root. Empty string or "/" means the root. For example "docs" or "src/components"
ai.tool.readFile.desc: Read the content of a text file in the project. Supports text files only (Markdown, code, config files, etc.). Content is automatically truncated beyond 4000 characters. Use to understand specific file contents.
ai.tool.readFile.filePath: File path relative to the project root. For example "README.md" or "docs/design/system-architecture.md"
ai.tool.getFileInfo.desc: Get metadata for a single file: name, type, size, and last modified time. Use to check if a file exists or compare file sizes without reading the full content.
ai.tool.getFileInfo.filePath: File path relative to the project root
ai.tool.moveFile.desc: Move or rename a file/directory from source to target. This operation is added to the approval queue and will only execute after user approval in the Approvals tab.
ai.tool.createFolder.desc: Create a new directory (can be nested). This operation is added to the approval queue and will only execute after user approval in the Approvals tab.
ai.tool.createFolder.folderPath: Directory path to create (relative to project root)
ai.tool.deleteFile.desc: Delete a file or empty directory (non-empty directories cannot be deleted). This operation is added to the approval queue and will only execute after user approval in the Approvals tab.
ai.tool.deleteFile.filePath: Path to delete (relative to project root)
ai.tool.batchOperations.desc: Execute multiple file operations (move_file/create_folder/delete_file) in a single batch. The batch is approved and executed as an atomic transaction; if any step fails, the entire batch is rolled back. This operation is added to the approval queue and requires user approval before execution.
ai.tool.batchOperations.operations: Array of operations, each with a type and corresponding path fields
ai.systemPrompt:
You are Doc77 AI Assistant, a professional local document management agent.

## Your Capabilities
1. **Browse projects** — Use list_files to inspect directory structures and understand project organization
2. **Read documents** — Use read_file to read file contents and understand project details
3. **Analyze and suggest** — Based on actual file content, provide suggestions for document organization, classification, and renaming
4. **Answer questions** — Answer user questions based on project file contents
5. **Propose file operations** — Use move_file / create_folder / delete_file / batch_operations to suggest file reorganization actions

## Operating Principles
1. **Look before you answer** — When users ask project-related questions, first use tools to examine the actual files, then answer based on facts. Do not guess.
2. **Concise with evidence** — Keep answers concise and reference specific file names and content as supporting evidence
3. **Be specific** — When suggesting file reorganization, use concrete file paths and operation descriptions
4. **Consistent language** — Always reply in English
5. **Write operations require approval** — When the user explicitly asks to organize/move/rename/delete files, call the corresponding write tools to submit operations.
   The tool will return a task ID and place the operation in the approval queue — **it will not actually execute until the user approves**.
   After submission, inform the user: the operation has been queued, what the task ID is, and to go to the Approvals tab to confirm. Multiple related operations should use batch_operations whenever possible.

## Security Constraints
- Do not attempt to read or manipulate sensitive files such as .env, .git, or key files (the system will reject these)
- All write operations only enter the queue; they will not execute until the user confirms in the Approvals tab — do not claim operations are "complete"
- Very large files (>50MB) should be handled manually
mcp.param.operations: Array of operations, each element contains type and corresponding parameters
mcp.tool.listFiles.desc: List files and folders in the specified path, including name/type/size/modified. Sensitive files are filtered by default.
mcp.tool.readFile.desc: Read file content (only within registered projects; sensitive files are automatically rejected)
mcp.tool.getFileInfo.desc: Get file metadata (size, modification time, type)
mcp.tool.writeFile.desc: Create or overwrite file content (requires approval)
mcp.tool.moveFile.desc: Move or rename a file (requires approval)
mcp.tool.deleteFile.desc: Delete a file or empty folder (requires mandatory approval)
mcp.tool.batchOperations.desc: Execute multiple operations in batch (approve as a whole, atomic rollback)
mcp.tool.getTaskStatus.desc: Query the status of a previously submitted write task

cli.banner.subtitle: Local document management Agent
cli.help.content:
Doc77 v{version} — Secure by default, conversation-driven intelligent local document management Agent Usage: doc77 [options] 📦 Desktop: https://github.com/xyy277/doc77/releases Core commands: start [--port ] [--bind ] [--no-browser] Start Web Dashboard discover [path] Scan and discover candidate projects discover --path ~/work Specify scan directory discover --depth 3 Specify scan depth (default 2) register [--name ] Register a project list [--json] List all projects remove Remove a project by ID update [--name ] [--path

] Update a project status Check service status --version Show version number --help Show help Module management: i Install optional modules rm Uninstall modules vendor-install [--translate ] [--mirror] [--no-pyodide] Download offline resources Configuration: config set Set config item config get Get config item config list List all config config set-password Set password config change-password Change password config reset-password Reset password (requires recovery code) config reset-password --force Force reset (clears encrypted data) config recovery-codes Regenerate recovery codes MCP Service: mcp serve [--http] [--port ] Start MCP service Task Approval: approve --list List pending tasks approve --accept Approve a task approve --reject Reject a task approve --accept --all Batch approve approve --reject --all Batch reject Lock Management: lock status View active locks lock release Manually release a lock AI Capabilities: ai summarize Summarize a document ai classify

Analyze project structure ai summary Generate project summary ai chat Enter chat mode

❌ Cannot interactively set password (non-TTY environment). Please run:
   doc77 config set-password
   Or: doc77 start (bind 127.0.0.1) and set password via Web

⚠️  Type "yes-i-know" to confirm:
